commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Gateway `chat.send` dispatch-rejection handling to preserve abort-first terminal results, keep error-first dispatch failures terminal, and adds focused Gateway chat regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +40, Tests +381. Total +421 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still has a source-level path where a post-dispatch rejection writes an error after an abort path has already made the run terminal, and the PR adds deterministic regression coverage for both race orderings.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the next action is maintainer review of the Gateway terminal precedence and merge-risk acceptance.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve abort after dispatch rejection
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Preserves the terminal `chat.abort` outcome when `chat.send` dispatch rejects after abort already completed.
- Preserves the real dispatch `error` outcome when abort arrives only during awaited error persistence, instead of letting a late abort mask/cache the error.
- Keeps current `main` dispatch-error lifecycle behavior intact: pre-agent dispatch errors still persist failed session state and emit `sessions.changed`.
- Adds regressions for both race orderings and prevents stale abort tombstones from leaking into later same-id retries.

Why does this matter now?

- `chat.send` caches terminal abort results for abort races. The post-dispatch rejection path still had two bad windows: abort-first could be overwritten by a late dispatch error, and error-first could expose `aborted` while the handler was awaiting fallback persistence.
- The conflict refresh also picked up the current `main` dispatch-error lifecycle path, so the merge has to preserve both the abort/error terminal-state contract and the session lifecycle contract.

What is the intended outcome?

- Same-run retry after an abort-first race observes the terminal timeout/aborted payload, not a synthetic dispatch error.
- Same-run retry after an error-first race observes the dispatch error while the error dedupe is live; a later retry can start normally after error dedupe cleanup.
- Late abort in the error-first path returns `aborted:false`, emits no aborted chat state, and does not recreate the abort tombstone.
- Pre-agent dispatch error continues to persist failed session lifecycle state and emit `sessions.changed` with `reason:"chat.dispatch-error"`.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Client-side terminal ACK handling.
- The original pre-dispatch attachment-preparation abort race, which is already fixed on `main`.

What does success look like?

- The PR diff stays limited to `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`.
- Focused Gateway regression, sibling lifecycle suite, typecheck, lint, diff hygiene, production-handler proof, and real Gateway/WebSocket RPC proof all pass on the refreshed head.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The `.catch` path snapshots whether abort had already won before any awaited fallback persistence.
- The error-winning path closes abortability, clears late abort state, and writes same-run error dedupe before awaiting fallback persistence.
- The session-lifecycle error event is still persisted/emitted after active-run cleanup.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Fixes #90999

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #84176 and #84306.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

Post-dispatch `chat.send` rejection could produce contradictory terminal state around `chat.abort`: abort-first could be overwritten by dispatch error, and error-first could briefly expose/cache `aborted` before final dispatch `error`. The conflict refresh also had to preserve current `main` dispatch-error lifecycle persistence.

- Real environment tested:

Linux source checkout at local conflict-repair head `0388ac3830a6215c738f518922d202ddbef23ff3`, Node/Vitest through repo scripts. The merge commit has parents `e16d6f5fcfeabe193b49883fe89061dead8efad1` and `9ff7abc8989c1770856b27d5b06e6bb6695ce666`. A pre-publish fetch advanced `upstream/main` to `258b83c4386cdaf2cee0d64af64d20bb09443347`; merge-tree stayed clean, no same-surface files changed on `main` since the merge base, and the PR-style diff stayed exactly the two Gateway files.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-prod-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD
OPENCLAW_PROBE_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr91000-conflict3-20260624T000548+0800.1817729 node --experimental-test-module-mocks --import tsx /tmp/pr91000-behavior-probe-dynamic.mts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91000-websocket-proof-dynamic.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal/proof captures from this branch:

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 86 passed (86)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 72 passed (72)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo
passed

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-prod-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo
passed

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
no output

$ git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD
clean
```

Production-handler proof on head `0388ac3830a6215c738f518922d202ddbef23ff3`:

```text
abort-first dispatch rejection: chat.abort returned aborted=true, dispatch saw AbortSignal.aborted=true, terminalDedupe.status=timeout, summary=aborted, chatBroadcastStates=[aborted], errorBroadcastCount=0
error-first late abort: chat.abort returned aborted=false, retryDuringPending.status=error with UNAVAILABLE, terminalDedupe.status=error, lateAbortTombstonePresentAfterError=false, midWindowChatBroadcastStates=[], chatBroadcastStates=[error], later retry starts
```

Real Gateway + WebSocket RPC proof on head `0388ac3830a6215c738f518922d202ddbef23ff3`:

```text
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 6 passed (6)
abort-first over WebSocket: chat.send ACK status=started; chat.abort response aborted=true; dispatch saw AbortSignal.aborted=true; chat broadcast states=[aborted]
error-first late abort over WebSocket: chat.send ACK status=started; late chat.abort response aborted=false; same-id retry during pending fallback returned cached status=error with UNAVAILABLE; mid-window chat broadcast states=[]; final chat broadcast states=[error]
```

- Observed result after fix:

Abort-first dispatch rejection preserves the terminal timeout/aborted result and emits only the aborted chat state. Error-first dispatch rejection makes the error terminal before awaited fallback persistence; late abort returns `aborted:false`, same-id retry during the pending fallback receives the cached error, no aborted chat state is emitted, and retry after deleting the error dedupe can start normally. The added lifecycle assertion confirms `sessions.changed` still reports `reason:"chat.dispatch-error"`, `status:"failed"`, and `hasActiveRun:false`.

- What was not tested:

No browser UI, native client, or live provider/model E2E run was performed. Transport proof uses a real Gateway server plus real WebSocket RPC client, controlling only the dispatch boundary through the repo test seam.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:

The race is deterministic once dispatch rejection, fallback persistence, and abort timing are controlled. Both probes control that boundary, avoiding flaky live-provider timing.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Earlier diagnostics on `main` reproduced both bad windows: abort-first ended with `[aborted,error]`; error-first late abort returned `aborted:true`, cached timeout/aborted during pending fallback, left a tombstone, and emitted `[aborted,error]`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-prod-pr91000-0388-rerun.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_PROBE_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr91000-conflict3-20260624T000548+0800.1817729 node --experimental-test-module-mocks --import tsx /tmp/pr91000-behavior-probe-dynamic.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91000-websocket-proof-dynamic.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added/updated coverage for abort-before-dispatch-reject preserving terminal abort state.
- Added/updated coverage for dispatch-reject-before-late-abort preserving terminal error state, rejecting late abort, serving cached error to same-id retry while fallback persistence is pending, and allowing a later retry after error dedupe cleanup.
- Added coverage that the current dispatch-error session lifecycle behavior still emits failed/no-active-run `sessions.changed` after the conflict refresh.
- Refreshed the branch against current `main`; the latest pre-publish fetch had no same-surface drift and clean merge-tree.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The original abort-first regression failed because `chat:<runId>` cached `status:"error"` instead of the expected timeout/aborted payload.
- The error-first probe found `chat.abort` could still return `aborted:true` during pending fallback persistence, and broadcasts could include both `aborted` and `error` for the same run.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Terminal abort/error ordering is now stable for post-dispatch rejection races.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Abort/error ordering in the `chat.send` async cleanup path, especially while fallback transcript persistence is pending, plus preserving dispatch-error session lifecycle persistence after active-run cleanup.

How is that risk mitigated?

Both race orderings are covered, the sibling session-lifecycle suite still passes, and the added lifecycle assertion checks the failed session notification emitted after active-run cleanup. The WebSocket proof exercises the real Gateway server/RPC transport with deterministic race timing.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the refreshed conflict-repair head and the updated real Gateway/WebSocket RPC proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

After branch/body refresh, CI and maintainer/bot review are pending.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No new bot comment was addressed. This updates exact base/head/proof evidence after conflict repair while preserving proof scope.
